TRANSCRIPT / BONUS / Promoting Other People’s Launches
Bonus - Promoting Other

Using Bonuses to Boost Sales

TRANSCRIPT / BONUS / Promoting Other People’s Launches
TRANSCRIPT
Okay, let's talk about bonus strategies for when you're promoting other people's
launches. This would be bonuses that you give to the people that buy through
your affiliate link or your joint venture link during a launch. It really can be during
any kind of promotion, but really we're talking about promoting other people's
launches here. This is basically you putting together a bonus package that someone
buys through your link. Then you will give them whatever … this bonus or bonuses.
This can be a great way to move the needle. It can be a great way to get people to
move because they might be looking ... you're introducing them to some other
product, some other offer. It's like, "If you buy right now, then you get this extra
bonus that I've put together for you." People love to get extra bonuses. There's a
lot of cool things you can do with these bonuses.
If you're competing with other affiliates or Joint Venture partners and maybe the
people who are on your list are also on the other Joint Venture partners lists, then
you're straight up competing against them. This could move the needle and get them
to buy through your link. Alternatively if you're the only person promoting it, then it
gives you just a great reason to promote. You can say, "Boy, I think this is a great
product and I've found one other really cool thing that I think will make this offer even
better. I put this bonus together for you. Go ahead and buy this thing right now and
I'll give you this bonus for free just by using my link." It can be a really great way to
move the needle. That's really what this video is about is how to put these bonuses
together and how to use them most effectively.
First of all, I always focus on being strategic in what you do. Just willy nilly, throwing
out different bonuses out there, you're not going to get the best results. I want
you to be really strategic. As you're thinking about this, I want you to think about
every time you put together a bonus, there's some cost to you to fulfill that bonus. It
might be a very, very minimal cost but there is some cost. Part of being strategic is
making sure you get the most results for the effort you expend. When you're
thinking about what kind of bonus to offer, you might want to think about what do
you already have that you could offer as a bonus? Do you have a product that's
already done that you could give as a bonus? Especially if it's a digital product, it’s
even better because then you'll have almost no marginal cost other than the cost to
actually administer the actual giving the bonus.
The way this whole thing works is usually after the launch is over, after the dust settles,
then whoever you're promoting, they will give you a list of all the email addresses, the
contact information for everyone who bought through your link. That way, that's how
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 8 --

TRANSCRIPT / BONUS / Promoting Other People’s Launches
you can actually send them the bonuses. There's two ways that it can go. Often there's
a control panel or something. You can log-in to see your affiliate sales and you can
actually access the contact information of the people you sent over directly. Some
systems will allow you to do that. Some actually won't give you the contact details.
Then you would go to the person that you're promoting for and say, "I have to fulfill
my bonuses. I need the contacts for the people who bought through my link." Then
you just send them the bonuses. The actual sending the bonuses will depend on the
format of the bonus and we'll get to that in a little bit.
One of the things is: What do you already have? What do you already have? What
products do you have? What interviews do you have? What videos do you have?
What's already done that you can give them and that won't require extra effort
from you? Next to think about is if you look around, you look at your inventory, what
you've got, and you're like, "This isn't good enough. I don't want to give these things.
The only thing I've got is my primary flagship offer and I don't want to give that away
because I don't want to hurt my positioning."
Then you might want to think about what do you want to create? Was there ever
a product that you just really wanted to create or a bonus that you really, really
wanted to create for your stuff? This way you're killing two birds with one stone
basically. You're saying, "I'm going to get to use this as a bonus and I'm going to get
to create this thing I wanted. In fact it's going to hold my feet to the fire because I've
been wanting to create this thing but I can't get started." This way you commit
yourself to actually creating this thing. What do you want to study? If there's some
topic that you just want to learn more about, that you want to become more proficient
at, you want to become better at, then one of the greatest ways to do that is to offer a
bonus for it. Then you have to knuckle down and study it and create something
around that topic.
Again, this is in that area of holding your feet to the fire. Another thing to think
about is how much time do you have? If you want to create some massive, huge
bonus but you're already stressed for time, you don't have much time, then it's going
to impact what you can create and what you should commit to creating. Next up is
how much time is it worth? If you're going to do this promotion and you're only
going to end up making one or two sales and you're going to make $40 in
commissions or something like that, then it's probably not going to be worth putting
a huge effort into it unless it answers one of those questions we just talked about, like
it was something you really, really wanted to create.
Now if it's a promotion you're going to do and you're going to make tens of
thousands of dollars, then it might be worth investing some ... a significant amount of
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 8 --

TRANSCRIPT / BONUS / Promoting Other People’s Launches
time into creating a bonus package that's really going to be fantastic and it'll help you
move the needle. It can be tough going into a promotion knowing exactly how it's
going to convert. One of the things you can do is you can start the promotion and
you can maybe offer a couple bonuses. We'll talk more about this later. We'll talk
about bonus stacking. Maybe you offer one or two bonuses and they're real easy
bonuses. You've already created them. All of a sudden, you notice this offer is really,
really converting. You're making lots of sales. Now you can sit back and say, "Wow,
we've got three days until the close. I'm really going to pile it on here because this
thing is converting like crazy and I can afford to invest more time and effort into this
and create a bigger, better bonus package."
Then another one is can it lead to a follow-up offer? This can be a case where
maybe you're delivering ... the way you deliver your bonus. Maybe it's a webinar or a
teleseminar or a coaching session. By virtue of the interaction that you create with
the people that you're delivering the bonus to, it just leads to a situation where
you can just make another offer. Maybe part of it is someone buys from you and
you're going to take part in three mastermind calls where you expound on the topic,
whatever the product is. It could be learning to play blues guitar. You offer three
mastermind sessions where you get together and you're going to answer questions.
You're all going to network and mastermind together.
Somewhere towards the end of those sessions, you say, "Boy, this mastermind has
been fantastic. If you guys want to continue with this, well I actually have this great
mastermind. You can go here for more details about it." There's a saying. "A buyer is a
buyer." Anyone who is a buyer is a much better prospect for you. It's much easier to
sell to someone who has already bought from you. In this case, they haven't bought
from you but they've bought because of your recommendation. That's almost as
powerful. Following up with an offer to someone like that can be very, very powerful
and your bonuses can lead into that.
Another one is: Can you chunk or bundle your bonuses? Let me explain what I
mean by that. Will you be able to use it for more than one promotion? I recently did
two promotions. I did them back to back for two different people, but they were not
for the same product. They were for somewhat similar products. They were in the
same county. They were in the same general area. What I was able to do was I
created one bonus and I offered it for both promotions. Let me show you in a little
more detail what that looked like. I had two promotions. Promotion one and
promotion two. For promotion one, I offered bonus A and B, just to keep this really
simple. I offered two bonuses. Bonus A was a bonus I had sitting around already. It
was very easy for me to offer that bonus.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 8 --

TRANSCRIPT / BONUS / Promoting Other People’s Launches
Then what I decided to do was I decided to create bonus B. Then a few weeks later, I
did promotion number two. Now I hadn't fulfilled on bonus B yet because usually
when you fulfill on your bonuses, you're typically delivering the bonus after the refund
period is over. It's just typical. It's not all the time but if someone buys for you or they
buy it from the promotion, you wait until that promotion ... until the refund period is
over. Then you give the bonus. This stops people from just going and buying every
product out there and collecting the bonuses and then refunding. In any case,
typically the delivery of your bonuses can be delayed by 30 days or even 45 days,
depending on how long the refund period is.
I offered bonus B in promotion one. Then a couple weeks later, I did promotion two. It
just so happened that bonus B was a great fit for promotion two as well. In promotion
two, I also offered bonus B. Then I added an additional bonus C, just because I
wanted the bonus packages to be different because I knew there would be people
that would buy promotion number one and promotion number two. I didn't want
them to look at the bonus package for promotion number two and say, "Well I've
already got bonus B so whatever." I wanted to be able to try to move that needle a
little bit. Actually I think in both of these, I actually offered three bonuses total
and only one bonus overlapped. This is a way you can get more bang for the
buck out of a given bonus.
Here, bonus B, I'm delivering it live. When you deliver bonuses live, it's just a little
more attractive. In this case, bonus B was a very attractive bonus but I was able to get
more than just one promotion out of it. I was able to get two promotions out of it. If
you're creating a new bonus, can you use this ... how can we build this bonus so we
use it for future promotions? One easy thing is say going back to that ... you're
doing a promotion for a launch about how to play blues guitar. Say you're recording a
video, say like this video. You might not want to say, "Hey, welcome everyone who
bought Joe Bob's Learning Blues Guitar. This is a bonus video just for you guys."
Don't do something like that, that will date it or make it so specific that you can't use it
for other promotions.
Okay, moving along. Question is: Do you need a bonus? You might not need a
bonus at all. This is the case where if you don't have any competition, then you
might not need it. Now even if you don't have competition, offering bonuses often
gives you a great reason why you're promoting, a great reason to mail. Even if you
don't have competition, you might still want to offer a bonus. Do you need it to push
people over the edge? Sometimes you might be promoting a launch and the offer is
so insanely great that you just need to tell people about it. You don't even need a
bonus to push people over the edge, but it's a good question to ask.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

TRANSCRIPT / BONUS / Promoting Other People’s Launches
How good is your connection story? This is a new concept, a connection story.
That's your connection to the product you're promoting. If you took the product and
went through it and created … it just had huge results for you in your life, then
that's a really strong connection story. Now if you've never looked at the product,
never thought about the product, never bought the product, then your connection
story isn't as good. Usually if your connection story is really, really good, it makes
it easier to promote and you might not need bonuses. Then will a bonus really help
your promotion? I've been hitting at this in numerous ways as we move through this,
but if your connection story is really great, maybe you don't need a bonus. If it's not
so great, you need a reason to keep on mailing your people, maybe a bonus really,
really move the needle.
What type of bonus should you offer? First of all, you want to make it relevant. If
it's about blues guitar, then offering free massages for a year might be a cool bonus
but it's not really relevant. It doesn't give you a great way to continue to talk about the
offer as you move through the launch. I like to make it relevant. A set of steak knives
for someone who is doing a mindfulness training, it's not relevant. Sure, maybe some
people might want some steak knives, but it's just not relevant. I like to make my
bonuses relevant. Look for ways to personalize it to the launch. This is just along the
lines of making it relevant. Making it really fit and personalized to the offer.
Look for ways to tie it to your products and your positioning. I teach about
product launches. Maybe I might be promoting a product about traffic. Maybe my
bonus can be about traffic within product launches. In other words, as I am doing this
promotion and talking about this offer and talking about this launch and talking about
the content of a launch, I always want to be building my positioning, whatever
that positioning is. I want to be able to talk about my positioning and my
products. Just look for ways to tie into your product or positioning.
This is a big one: look for gaps in the product. Going back to the blues guitar, if it
was a product about playing blues guitar ... say it was about blues lead guitar. It's
about playing lead guitar but there wasn't anything in there about rhythm guitar. The
reality is if you're playing blues, you need to … yeah, you might be playing lead but
you also have to play rhythm. Maybe you could create a product or a video or
something about playing rhythm guitar when you're playing blues guitar. Look for
gaps in the product. What could expand the product? What is missing from the
product? That can be a really, really, really powerful type of bonus.
Okay. Now I'm just going to do a quick bonus brainstorm. I'm going to just walk
through and name some of these for you. This is just a list just to get you thinking. A
great bonus is consulting that goes with the product. The consulting could be
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 8 --

TRANSCRIPT / BONUS / Promoting Other People’s Launches
group consulting, where you do a series of group calls, group Q&A calls, group
mastermind calls, group coaching calls. They can also be one-on-one. You can say,
"Boy, if you buy through my link then I'll do a one-on-one e consultation with you," or
two consultations or three. However many you want. Then you can also do this thing I
call office hours. I've done this where basically you say, "I'm going to have office
hours. You can talk to me one on one. I'm going to set aside a chunk of time on X
number of days and you can just call in and talk to me."
What this does is it gives you the benefit of one-on-one, but you're probably not
going to be talking to every single person because not every single person is going
to want to book an appointment with you. To me it's just a little easier to do office
hours than to book a bunch of one-on-ones because when you're booking one-on-
one calls, they might not all line up. I did this last week. I offered office hours. I did two
different days. I did three hours each day. I had a ton of fun. It was great. I didn't have
appointments all over my calendar. It's just another way really to offer one-on-one
without having to set up a bunch of one-on-one consultations. That's just consulting
to go with the product.
Q&A call. This is very similar to consulting. Consulting, you might be more where
you're actually digging into someone's business and you're helping them apply. A
Q&A call could just be we're going to answer questions about this product. The Q&A
call, it could be with you or it could be with you and the product creator. Often during
a launch, you can go to the creator or the producer, whoever is doing the launch. You
go to them and say, "Hey, can we do a Q&A call just for the people who buy through
my link?" Most of the time they're going to say yes.
A product of yours. We talked about this a little bit up above. It could be a product
that's already existing or it could be a product that you're going to create. One
thing I'd be careful of is just not giving away your best flagship product because when
you start to give away products ... if someone bought from you last week and this
week you're giving it away, that just doesn't create great feeling for anyone and it's
not good for your positioning.
Next is a live event custom-created for the promotion, for the launch. I've done
this in the past where I offered an implementation day where I promoted someone. I
said, "If you buy through my link, I'm going to do a live implementation day." I did
them here in Durango where I had people flying to Durango. Also one time when part
of the offer, part of the launch offer was a live event, I said, "I'm going to come to that
live event and a day after the live event, I'm going to do implementation day. If you
buy through my link, you can come and be there with me. There's only going to be
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 8 --

TRANSCRIPT / BONUS / Promoting Other People’s Launches
a few people there. It's going to be a small meeting and it's going to be really, really
powerful."
You could also have a ticket to a live event that you're already doing. In the other
case, you're creating a custom live event just for the people who bought through that
link. This case is if you do live events, then you can say, "Wow, you can get a ticket to
my live event." I've done this on very, very, very rare occasions where I have given
someone a ticket to an event I'm already doing if they bought for a launch through
my affiliate link. Or webinar training on a related topic. This is along the lines of ...
up above I talked about what gap is there in the product? Instead of going and
creating a big product about it, just do a webinar about whatever is missing, whatever
the gap is. Maybe something's not missing but there's something that's just related.
There's a related topic that you think of. Do a webinar on it and boom. It's just a really
easy bonus to do. You schedule it. You get on. You do a 60-minute, 90-minute webinar
and you're done. Really easy.
A mastermind to go through the product via a teleseminar or webinar. I've done
this before where I said, "Boy, I'm going to be going through this product at the same
time you guys are. I'm really excited about this product. What I've decided to do is
I've decided to do a mastermind. What we're going to do is we're going to do a
webinar or a series of webinars or a teleseminar. We're going to get on and I'm going
to walk through what I've learned, but I also want to hear from you about what you've
learned because all of us together are a lot smarter than any one of us at one time."
Or in-person mastermind. Very similar to the above but this is where you would
actually get together physically and do it.
Done-for-you services. This could be where you're actually helping implement the
product. Now this is super high value and it's super high touch. It could take a lot of
your time so you have to be careful with this one. It really does have to be worth it for
you to do something like that.
That was a quick bonus brainstorm. Another great way to do this is get on the list of
other people in your market or even outside of your market. Find the people that do
promote launches and just watch what kind of bonus offers they give. Say, "Boy, how
can I apply that? How can I use that?" That's real quick on basically bonuses within a
launch. Like I said when I started, this is a really great way to move the needle when
you're promoting someone else's launch.
